quotes.quote: The LLM produces Python code, which is then executed and scored on a sandbox. Searching over strategies dramatically increases performance: The system uses the score together with output logs and other information to hill climb towards a better score.
quotes.rationale: Sandbox execution produces scores and logs that are routed back into candidate improvement, an implemented computational scoring channel.

quotes.quote: A scorable task, together with research ideas proposing methods to solve the task, are fed to an LLM, which produces code to evaluate the scorable task in a sandbox. This is then embedded within a tree search algorithm, whereby new nodes are chosen balancing exploitation and exploration, sampling from the LLM (Methods).
quotes.rationale: Confirms the scorable-task sandbox evaluation is embedded in the search loop.

quotes.quote: The node with the globally maximum PUCT𝑖 score is selected. The language model then generates a single novel child solution conditioned on this parent’s code and score. The new code is executed, assigned an empirical score, and appended to the search tree, followed by a backpropagation of the visit count to its ancestors. This global, flat-tree structure allows the system to seamlessly backtrack and branch from any historical node if the current optimization path plateaus.
quotes.rationale: A deterministic PUCT rule ranks nodes and controls expansion, a distinct search-control mechanism from raw sandbox scoring.

quotes.quote: approach does not rely on beam search (which aggressively prunes unselected candidates at each depth layer) nor a (1 + 𝜆 ) evolution strategy (which maintains only the most recent generation and discards historical states).
quotes.rationale: Specifies the retention policy of the search controller: historical states are not pruned or discarded.

quotes.quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).
quotes.rationale: Human experts audited whether generated code matched the requested algorithms; reported as a post hoc confirmation, not a loop input.

diagnostics.consequence: Search optimizes toward a score whose fidelity is unverified, so gains may reflect metric exploitation rather than genuine method improvement.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The LLM produces Python code, which is then executed and scored on a sandbox. Searching over strategies dramatically increases performance: The system uses the score together with output logs and other information to hill climb towards a better score.

diagnostics.statement: The in-loop sandbox scoring channel that drives hill climbing has no reported reliability study: no evidence is given that the empirical score is a valid or unbiased proxy for the intended research quality.

diagnostics.consequence: Specification non-compliance can only be detected after a run completes, so non-adherent solutions remain admissible to the tree and can be selected and propagated during search.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).

The node with the globally maximum PUCT𝑖 score is selected. The language model then generates a single novel child solution conditioned on this parent’s code and score. The new code is executed, assigned an empirical score, and appended to the search tree, followed by a backpropagation of the visit count to its ancestors. This global, flat-tree structure allows the system to seamlessly backtrack and branch from any historical node if the current optimization path plateaus.

diagnostics.statement: The only human validation reported is post hoc expert inspection of code adherence; it is terminal and does not feed back into node selection or search control.

diagnostics.consequence: Ideation quality is unvalidated within the reported system, so claims about research contribution rest only on task scores.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A scorable task, together with research ideas proposing methods to solve the task, are fed to an LLM, which produces code to evaluate the scorable task in a sandbox. This is then embedded within a tree search algorithm, whereby new nodes are chosen balancing exploitation and exploration, sampling from the LLM (Methods).

diagnostics.statement: The packet reports no evaluation of novelty, correctness, or significance of the research ideas fed into the system; all reported evaluation targets executability, score, and algorithmic adherence.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The LLM produces Python code, which is then executed and scored on a sandbox. Searching over strategies dramatically increases performance: The system uses the score together with output logs and other information to hill climb towards a better score.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report a property-specific validation of the sandbox score itself, for example independent held-out evaluation of top-scoring nodes, to test whether score improvements track genuine task performance rather than metric exploitation.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Reported rank correlation or score gap, with any degradation on held-out evaluation explicitly quantified
success_check.evidence_needed: A published validation table or figure comparing in-loop and independent scores for the same solutions
success_check.observable: Agreement between in-loop sandbox score and an independent held-out evaluation for top-ranked solutions

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).

recommendations.proposed_action: Route an automated specification-compliance check into node admission or selection so that adherence to the requested algorithm gates the search tree rather than being assessed only after the run.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Reduced non-adherence rate relative to the current post hoc inspection baseline reported in Extended Data Table 1
success_check.evidence_needed: Ablation comparing expert-inspection adherence rates for gated versus ungated runs
success_check.observable: Rate of non-adherent implementations retained in the search tree with and without the in-loop compliance gate

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A scorable task, together with research ideas proposing methods to solve the task, are fed to an LLM, which produces code to evaluate the scorable task in a sandbox. This is then embedded within a tree search algorithm, whereby new nodes are chosen balancing exploitation and exploration, sampling from the LLM (Methods).

recommendations.proposed_action: State explicitly whether any evaluation of the input research ideas occurs before they enter the coding and search loop, and describe its mechanism and routing if one exists.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Methods text states either a named idea-stage evaluator with its decision role or that no such evaluator is used
success_check.evidence_needed: Revised methods section identifying the ideation-stage evaluator and its feedback routing
success_check.observable: Presence of an explicit description of idea-stage filtering or its documented absence

memory_structure: global flat search tree of all historical solution nodes with code, empirical scores, and visit counts
no pruning of unselected candidates and no discarding of historical states
policy_type: PUCT-based tree search over solution nodes balancing exploitation and exploration
score-and-log-conditioned hill climbing via LLM sampling
